highlight.title: Next.js 16 Lands with Cache Components and Turbopack
highlight.summary: <cite index="11-1,11-8,11-24">Next.js 16 introduces Cache Components with explicit "use cache" directives, making caching opt-in rather than implicit. Turbopack becomes the stable default bundler, providing 2-5x faster builds.</cite> <cite index="11-28,15-22">This marks a fundamental shift from the unpredictable caching of Next.js 15 to explicit control over what gets cached.</cite>

resources.title: Critical Security Updates for React Server Components
resources.why_now: <cite index="9-4,9-7,9-8">A critical vulnerability (CVE-2025-66478) in React Server Components allows remote code execution, affecting standard Next.js applications without code modifications.</cite> Immediate patching required for production apps.

resources.title: React 19 Server Components Performance Investigation
resources.why_now: <cite index="34-20,34-27">This deep-dive analysis compares Client-Side Rendering, SSR, and RSC performance patterns, answering whether Server Components are worth it from a performance perspective.</cite> Essential reading as teams evaluate RSC adoption.

resources.title: TypeScript Patterns Every React Developer Should Know in 2025
resources.why_now: <cite index="26-2,26-10">Based on migrating large-scale applications with millions of users to TypeScript, this covers patterns that significantly improve code quality and developer experience.</cite> The React ecosystem has standardized on TypeScript patterns.

resources.title: Next.js 16.2: Faster Startup and React Upgrades
resources.why_now: <cite index="16-8,16-9">Next.js 16.2 delivers 87% faster startup times and includes React upgrades that make Server Components payload deserialization up to 350% faster.</cite> These performance wins are shipping now in production.

claude_take: The Next.js/React ecosystem is maturing rapidly with explicit caching models replacing magic, while TypeScript patterns are becoming standardized across teams. However, the recent RSC security vulnerability highlights that bleeding-edge adoption requires careful risk assessment - sometimes the performance gains aren't worth the architectural complexity and vendor lock-in.
